title: Checkout customer details form and admin orders view

requirements.text: Extend the backend `main.mo` to store customer orders with delivery details. Add an `Order` type (or extend the existing one) that includes: customer full name, email address, phone number, delivery address, list of ordered items (product name, quantity, unit price), order total, and a timestamp. Add an `placeOrder` update function that accepts cart items plus customer details and persists the order. Add a `getOrders` query function that returns all stored orders. Ensure data persists across upgrades using stable storage.

quotes: when you proceed checkout it asks for your details so we can get it to you
put in the admin panel a thing so we can see it and get it to you

acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes a `placeOrder` update function accepting customer details (name, email, phone, address) and cart items.
Backend exposes a `getOrders` query function returning all placed orders.
Orders persist across canister upgrades via stable storage.
Each order record includes customer name, email, phone, delivery address, ordered items, total price, and a creation timestamp.

requirements.text: Replace the simulated order placement in `frontend/src/pages/CheckoutPage.tsx` with a multi-step checkout flow. Before showing the order summary, display a customer details form with fields for: Full Name, Email Address, Phone Number, and Delivery Address (street, city, postcode). All fields are required. On form submission, validate inputs and then call the backend `placeOrder` mutation passing both the cart contents and the customer details. On success, clear the cart and show a confirmation screen with the order details and a message that it will be delivered to the provided address.

quotes: when you proceed checkout it asks for your details so we can get it to you

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Proceed to Checkout' from the cart navigates to a customer details form.
The form includes fields for Full Name, Email, Phone Number, Street Address, City, and Postcode.
All fields are required; the form shows inline validation errors if any field is empty on submit.
On successful submission, the backend `placeOrder` function is called with cart items and customer details.
On success, the cart is cleared and a confirmation screen is shown displaying the delivery address.
On error, an error message is displayed and the form remains editable.
The form and confirmation screen follow the existing dark gaming visual theme.

requirements.text: Add a `placeOrder` mutation hook and a `getOrders` query hook to `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts`. The `placeOrder` mutation must call the backend `placeOrder` function with customer details and cart item data, await the result, and invalidate the `orders` cache key on success. The `getOrders` query must call the backend `getOrders` function and return the list of all orders.

quotes: make it so when you proceed checkout it asks for your details so we can get it to you

acceptanceCriteria: `useOrders` query hook fetches all orders from the backend via `getOrders`.
`usePlaceOrder` mutation hook calls the backend `placeOrder` function with the correct argument types.
On mutation success, the `orders` React Query cache key is invalidated so the admin panel re-fetches.
Price/BigInt fields are serialised correctly when passed to the backend.

requirements.text: Add an 'Orders' tab to the Admin Panel in `frontend/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx` (alongside the existing Products and Sponsors tabs). This tab must display a list of all placed orders fetched via the `getOrders` query. For each order show: order ID/number, customer name, email, phone, full delivery address, a line-item list of products ordered with quantities and prices, the order total, and the order date/time. Style the orders list consistently with the existing dark gaming admin theme. The tab must be visible and accessible after the admin password gate is passed.

quotes: put in the admin panel a thing so we can see it and get it to you

acceptanceCriteria: An 'Orders' tab appears in the Admin Panel alongside Products and Sponsors tabs.
The Orders tab is only visible after passing the PIN '6280' password gate.
Each order card/row shows: customer name, email, phone, delivery address, ordered items with quantities and prices, total, and timestamp.
If no orders exist, a friendly empty-state message is displayed.
The orders list is scrollable and follows the existing dark gaming visual theme.
Orders data is fetched live from the backend using the `getOrders` query hook.

constraints: Do not modify files listed under frontend.immutablePaths.
Password validation for the admin panel must remain frontend-only (PIN '6280').
All Motoko logic must remain in the single actor in backend/main.mo.
Generate or update migration.mo if stable state types change due to order storage additions.
nonGoals: Payment processing or integration with any payment gateway.
User authentication for customers (orders are anonymous/guest).
Order status updates or fulfilment tracking beyond storing the order.
Deleting or editing existing orders from the admin panel.
